name: Battle Keywords
source: Core Rules pp.36-47, 114-118 (combat terms and page references)

keywords.definition: Roll 1d6 per crew member. Result <= Reactions stat = Quick Action this round.
Crew members who passed their Reaction Roll act first. Each can Move + Act.
Crew members who failed their Reaction Roll act after enemies. Each can Move + Act.
All enemy figures act between Quick and Slow Actions. See AI behavior type.
Check morale (if casualties this round), resolve conditions, check battle events.
Move up to full Speed in inches. Cannot fire weapons this activation.
Move up to half Speed (round up). Can fire weapons after moving.
Move 1" in any direction as a free action when enemy enters brawling range. Cannot Bail if Stunned.
+1 to Hit. Requires not moving this activation. Stacks with other modifiers.
Fire at an enemy that moves through line of sight during their activation. -1 to Hit.
When 2+ crew fire at same target in one round, second and subsequent shots get +1 to Hit.
Target in cover: -1 to Hit. Must be behind terrain that blocks at least 50% of the figure.
Draw imaginary line from firing figure's head to any part of target. If unobstructed, target is visible.
Close combat when figures are within 1". Both roll 1d6 + Combat Skill. Highest wins. Loser takes a hit.
When a figure is hit: roll weapon Damage vs target Toughness. If Damage >= Toughness, figure is a casualty.
Figure removed from play. Crew casualties roll on Injury Table after battle.
Figure is Stunned: cannot act next activation. If Stunned again while Stunned, becomes a casualty.
Some armor grants a save roll. Roll 1d6: if result >= save value, hit is negated.
When first casualty occurs each round, enemy checks morale. Roll 2d6 vs Morale value.
Failed morale: 1d3 enemies flee the battlefield immediately. Remove from play.
At battle start, roll 1d6. On 6 (modified by crew size), crew acts before deployment.
Maximum distance in inches a weapon can fire. Targets beyond range cannot be hit.
Number of attack rolls per activation with this weapon. Each shot is resolved separately.
Roll this value vs target Toughness when a hit is scored. Damage >= Toughness = casualty.
Cannot advance toward enemy. Can only fire at -1 to Hit or take cover.
Crew member injured in battle. Roll on Injury Table after battle to determine severity.
Random event on rounds 2 and 4. Roll d100 on the Battle Events Table.
After round 4: roll d6 each round. On 1-2 battle ends. On 6 escalation event occurs.
Additional enemies arrive at table edge. Place d6 figures at a random board edge.
Area where figures are initially placed. Standard: 6" from your table edge.
Roll d100 before battle. May encounter unique enemies or situations.
Mission goal on the battlefield. Must be reached and held to complete mission objectives.
